text.title: Distribution Channel Research Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real deployments of Distribution Channel Research. Each ties a symptom to a root cause and a concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Founder picks Google Ads + Facebook Ads because 'that is what everyone does'.
antipattern.fix: Force-consider all 19 channels; reject 16 with one-liner each.

antipattern.symptom: <=$5k spread across 6 channels; no channel reaches statistical signal.
antipattern.fix: Cap shortlist at 3; merge remaining ideas into a parking lot.

antipattern.symptom: Test runs 4 weeks past $500 cap because 'it is almost working'.
antipattern.fix: Hard-stop at $500; archive the test even if results look promising.

antipattern.symptom: Channel with lowest CAC picked; D30 retention 8%; LTV:CAC collapses.
antipattern.fix: Score 4 axes; require D30 retention >= 40% for any locked channel.
